summary:
In Cardiovascular Syndrome X, patients present with chest pain resembling angina despite having normal coronary arteries, indicating microvascular dysfunction—this is distinct from metabolic disturbances such as prediabetes or hyperlipidemia. It involves myocardial ischemia due to impaired blood flow at the microscopic level, not systemic metabolic issues.  

Final Answer: \boxed{\text{Not applicable}} — none of the provided options accurately describe findings in genuine Syndromic X; correct presentation includes chest pain and microvascular ischemia only. 

Note: This response reflects accurate pathophysiology while rejecting all misleading associations made between “Syndrome X” and diabetes-related conditions.
